children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary may assign, and a State may assume, responsibility for determining whether certain designated activities are included within classes of action identified in regulation by the Secretary that are categorically excluded from requirements for environmental assessments or environmental impact statements pursuant to regulations promulgated by the Council on Environmental Quality under part 1500 of title 40, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on ).October 1, 2003

children.heading: .—Scope of authority
children.label: 2 .—Scope of authority
children.text: A determination described in paragraph (1) shall be made by a State in accordance with criteria established by the Secretary and only for types of activities specifically designated by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Criteria
children.label: 3 .—Criteria
children.text: The criteria under paragraph (2) shall include provisions for public availability of information consistent with and the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.).section 552 of title 542 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: .—Preservation of flexibility
children.label: 4 .—Preservation of flexibility
children.text: The Secretary shall not require a State, as a condition of assuming responsibility under this section, to forego project delivery methods that are otherwise permissible for highway projects.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: If a State assumes responsibility under subsection (a), the Secretary may also assign and the State may assume all or part of the responsibilities of the Secretary for environmental review, consultation, or other related actions required under any Federal law applicable to activities that are classified by the Secretary as categorical exclusions, with the exception of government-to-government consultation with Indian tribes, subject to the same procedural and substantive requirements as would be required if that responsibility were carried out by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Sole responsibility
children.label: 2 .—Sole responsibility
children.text: A State that assumes responsibility under paragraph (1) with respect to a Federal law shall be solely responsible and solely liable for complying with and carrying out that law, and the Secretary shall have no such responsibility or liability.

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: 1 .—In general
children.text: The Secretary and the State, after providing public notice and opportunity for comment, shall enter into a memorandum of understanding setting forth the responsibilities to be assigned under this section and the terms and conditions under which the assignments are made, including establishment of the circumstances under which the Secretary would reassume responsibility for categorical exclusion determinations.

children.label: A
children.text: assuming responsibility under subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: developing a memorandum of understanding under this subsection; or

children.label: C
children.text: addressing a responsibility in need of corrective action under subsection (d)(1)(B).

children.label: A
children.text: except as provided under subparagraph (C), shall have a term of not more than 3 years;

children.label: B
children.text: shall be renewable; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall have a term of 5 years, in the case of a State that has assumed the responsibility for categorical exclusions under this section for not fewer than 10 years.

children.heading: .—Acceptance of jurisdiction
children.label: 4 .—Acceptance of jurisdiction
children.text: In a memorandum of understanding, the State shall consent to accept the jurisdiction of the Federal courts for the compliance, discharge, and enforcement of any responsibility of the Secretary that the State assumes.

children.label: A
children.text: monitor compliance by the State with the memorandum of understanding and the provision by the State of financial resources to carry out the memorandum of understanding; and

children.label: B
children.text: take into account the performance by the State when considering renewal of the memorandum of understanding.

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary determines that the State is not adequately carrying out the responsibilities assigned to the State;

children.label: i
children.text: a notification of the determination of noncompliance;

children.label: ii
children.text: a period of not less than 120 days to take such corrective action as the Secretary determines to be necessary to comply with the applicable agreement; and

children.label: iii
children.text: on request of the Governor of the State, a detailed description of each responsibility in need of corrective action regarding an inadequacy identified under subparagraph (A); and

children.label: C
children.text: the State, after the notification and period described in clauses (i) and (ii) of subparagraph (B), fails to take satisfactory corrective action, as determined by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Termination by the state
children.label: 2 .—Termination by the state
children.text: The State may terminate the participation of the State in the program at any time by providing to the Secretary a notice not later than the date that is 90 days before the date of termination, and subject to such terms and conditions as the Secretary may provide.

children.heading: .—State Agency Deemed to Be Federal Agency
children.label: e .—State Agency Deemed to Be Federal Agency
children.text: A State agency that is assigned a responsibility under a memorandum of understanding shall be deemed to be a Federal agency for the purposes of the Federal law under which the responsibility is exercised.

children.heading: .—Legal Fees
children.label: f .—Legal Fees
children.text: A State assuming the responsibilities of the Secretary under this section for a specific project may use funds apportioned to the State under section 104(b)(2) for attorney’s fees directly attributable to eligible activities associated with the project.
